items.title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability
items.summary: In late 2025, an unknown threat actor exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in KnowledgeDeliver, a Learning Management System widely used in Japan. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5426, allowed unauthenticated remote code execution through ViewState deserialization attacks. The issue stemmed from identical hardcoded ASP.NET machine keys distributed across multiple customer deployments

items.title: USN-8299-1: Rclone vulnerabilities
items.summary:
It was discovered that Rclone incorrectly handled authorization in the remote
control API. An attacker could possibly use this issue to obtain sensitive
information. (CVE-2026-41176)

It was discovered that Rclone incorrectly handled backend instantiation via the
remote control API. An attacker could possibly use this issue to execute
arbitrary code. This issue only affected Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Ubuntu 25.10 and
Ubuntu 26.04 LTS. (CVE-2026-41179)

a fixed 1024-byte stack buffer without bounds checking. When qlog was

arbitrary code.
